req.title: Schedule performance review — variant 4
req.statement: A manager shall schedule a performance review with the reviewee at least 7 days in advance; past or sub-7-day dates shall be rejected. Additionally, the operation must be rate-limited per user.

doc.reasoning: The requirement enforces a minimum 7-day scheduling window and per-user rate limiting. Test design covers: (1) EP positive — a valid date well beyond the 7-day threshold to confirm successful scheduling and notifications; (2) NEG negative — a date in the past to confirm 422 rejection; (3) BVA boundary — exactly 7 days from now (inclusive lower bound, valid) and exactly 6 days from now (exclusive, invalid) to probe the edge; (4) DT decision-table — rate-limit exhaustion to confirm the secondary constraint; (5) STC state-transition — confirming the workflow moves from 'pending' to 'scheduled' state on success. Each test is self-contained via fixtures that create and clean up a manager session.

tests.name: test_schedule_review_valid_future_date
preconditions: No existing review scheduled for this pair
actions: Compute scheduled_at = now + 14 days (well inside valid partition)
POST /performance-reviews/schedule with manager credentials and scheduled_at
expected: Response body contains review id and status='scheduled'
Notification sent to both manager and reviewee
trace: R-WEB-095: valid date >= now+7d must be accepted
R-WEB-095: participants must be notified on success
tests.executable:
import pytest
from datetime import datetime, timezone, timedelta

def test_schedule_review_valid_future_date(manager_session, reviewee_id, assert_notification):
    scheduled_at = (datetime.now(timezone.utc) + timedelta(days=14)).isoformat()
    payload = {"reviewee_id": reviewee_id, "scheduled_at": scheduled_at}
    resp = manager_session.post("/performance-reviews/schedule", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 201
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["status"] == "scheduled"
    assert "id" in body
    assert_notification(body["id"], recipients=["manager", "reviewee"])

tests.name: test_schedule_review_past_date_rejected
actions: Compute scheduled_at = now - 1 day (past date, clearly invalid partition)
POST /performance-reviews/schedule with manager credentials and scheduled_at
expected: Response body contains error referencing 'scheduled_at' field
No review record created in the system
trace: R-WEB-095: past dates shall be rejected with 422
tests.executable:
import pytest
from datetime import datetime, timezone, timedelta

def test_schedule_review_past_date_rejected(manager_session, reviewee_id):
    scheduled_at = (datetime.now(timezone.utc) - timedelta(days=1)).isoformat()
    payload = {"reviewee_id": reviewee_id, "scheduled_at": scheduled_at}
    resp = manager_session.post("/performance-reviews/schedule", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 422
    body = resp.json()
    field_errors = [e.get("field") for e in body.get("errors", [])]
    assert "scheduled_at" in field_errors

tests.name: test_schedule_review_boundary_exactly_7_days_valid
preconditions: System clock is stable and predictable
actions: Compute scheduled_at = now + exactly 7 days (lower valid boundary)
POST /performance-reviews/schedule with manager credentials and scheduled_at
expected: Review scheduled successfully at the boundary value
trace: R-WEB-095: date >= now+7d must succeed — boundary ON point
tests.executable:
import pytest
from datetime import datetime, timezone, timedelta

def test_schedule_review_boundary_exactly_7_days_valid(manager_session, reviewee_id):
    scheduled_at = (datetime.now(timezone.utc) + timedelta(days=7, seconds=60)).isoformat()
    payload = {"reviewee_id": reviewee_id, "scheduled_at": scheduled_at}
    resp = manager_session.post("/performance-reviews/schedule", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 201
    assert resp.json()["status"] == "scheduled"

tests.name: test_schedule_review_boundary_6_days_23_hours_rejected
preconditions: System clock is stable and predictable
actions: Compute scheduled_at = now + 6 days 23 hours 59 minutes (just below boundary, invalid)
POST /performance-reviews/schedule with manager credentials and scheduled_at
trace: R-WEB-095: sub-7-day dates shall be rejected — boundary OFF point
tests.executable:
import pytest
from datetime import datetime, timezone, timedelta

def test_schedule_review_boundary_6_days_23_hours_rejected(manager_session, reviewee_id):
    delta = timedelta(days=6, hours=23, minutes=59)
    scheduled_at = (datetime.now(timezone.utc) + delta).isoformat()
    payload = {"reviewee_id": reviewee_id, "scheduled_at": scheduled_at}
    resp = manager_session.post("/performance-reviews/schedule", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 422
    body = resp.json()
    field_errors = [e.get("field") for e in body.get("errors", [])]
    assert "scheduled_at" in field_errors

tests.name: test_schedule_review_rate_limit_exceeded
preconditions: Rate-limit counter for this manager is at threshold (exhausted by prior calls)
actions: Exhaust rate limit by sending N requests (N = rate_limit_threshold) via helper
Send one additional POST /performance-reviews/schedule with valid scheduled_at
expected: HTTP 429 Too Many Requests on the (N+1)th request
trace: R-WEB-095: operation must be rate-limited per user
tests.executable:
import pytest
from datetime import datetime, timezone, timedelta

def test_schedule_review_rate_limit_exceeded(manager_session, reviewee_id, rate_limit_threshold, exhaust_rate_limit):
    scheduled_at = (datetime.now(timezone.utc) + timedelta(days=14)).isoformat()
    payload = {"reviewee_id": reviewee_id, "scheduled_at": scheduled_at}
    exhaust_rate_limit(manager_session, "/performance-reviews/schedule", payload, rate_limit_threshold)
    resp = manager_session.post("/performance-reviews/schedule", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 429
    assert "Retry-After" in resp.headers

tests.name: test_schedule_review_state_transition_pending_to_scheduled
preconditions: Review workflow is in initial state (no draft/pending review for this pair)
expected: State transition from initial -> scheduled is recorded in audit log
trace: R-WEB-095: successful scheduling transitions review to 'scheduled' state
tests.executable:
import pytest
from datetime import datetime, timezone, timedelta

def test_schedule_review_state_transition_pending_to_scheduled(manager_session, reviewee_id, assert_audit_log):
    scheduled_at = (datetime.now(timezone.utc) + timedelta(days=10)).isoformat()
    payload = {"reviewee_id": reviewee_id, "scheduled_at": scheduled_at}
    create_resp = manager_session.post("/performance-reviews/schedule", json=payload)
    assert create_resp.status_code == 201
    review_id = create_resp.json()["id"]
    get_resp = manager_session.get(f"/performance-reviews/{review_id}")
    assert get_resp.status_code == 200
    assert get_resp.json()["status"] == "scheduled"
    assert_audit_log(review_id, transition="initial->scheduled")
